solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int maxPalindromes(String s, int k) {
        int n = s.length();
        boolean[][] isPalindrome = new boolean[n][n];

        // Precompute all palindromic substrings
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            for (int l = 0, r = i; r < n; l++, r++) {
                if (l == r) isPalindrome[l][r] = true;
                else if (s.charAt(l) == s.charAt(r)) {
                    if (l + 1 > r - 1 || isPalindrome[l + 1][r - 1]) {
                        isPalindrome[l][r] = true;
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        int[] dp = new int[n + 1];

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            dp[i + 1] = dp[i];
            for (int j = i - k + 1; j >= 0; j--) {
                if (isPalindrome[j][i]) {
                    dp[i + 1] = Math.max(dp[i + 1], dp[j] + 1);
                }
            }
        }

        return dp[n];
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(n^2)
energy implications: MEDIUM
explanation: The solution precomputes all palindromic substrings using dynamic programming and then uses another DP array to find the maximum number of non-overlapping palindromic substrings of length at least k. The key strength is the efficient precomputation of palindromes, while the weakness is the O(n^2) space and time complexity, which is acceptable for n ≤ 2000.
